items.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro: A smarter model for your most complex tasks
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google's Gemini API has seen significant updates through early 2026. The Gemini 3 series has expanded with the release of Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview, which achieved a verified 77.1% score on the ARC-AGI-2 benchmark — more than double the performance of Gemini 3 Pro. Alongside it, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview launched as a cost-efficient option at $0.25/1M input tokens, targeting high-volume workloads. New capabilities include Grounding with Google Maps for Gemini 3 models, a multimodal embedding mo

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro | Generative AI on Vertex AI Documentation
items.author: docs.cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has made several major AI and Gemini announcements in early 2026. The flagship release is Gemini 3.1 Pro, described as a noticeably smarter and more capable baseline for complex problem-solving. It features a 1M token context window supporting text, audio, images, video, PDFs, and entire code repositories. It includes improved software engineering and agentic capabilities, more efficient thinking, and expanded thinking levels (including a new MEDIUM tier). The model is available in

items.title: Interactions API | Gemini API | Google AI for Developers
items.author: ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API offers two complementary mechanisms for structured responses: Function Calling and Structured Outputs. Function Calling allows the model to invoke developer-defined tools by returning structured JSON with a function name, arguments, and a unique ID — enabling multi-step agentic workflows where the model connects to external systems. Structured Outputs, on the other hand, constrains the model's final response to a provided JSON Schema, ensuring type-safe, predictable output. The Go

items.title: Google AI Studio Build Mode: The Free AI Coder That Beats Copilot & Cursor
items.author: medium.com
items.snippet: Google AI Studio launched a full-stack 'vibe coding' experience in early 2026, marking one of its most significant updates since launch. The platform now supports building complete applications — client-side React frontends paired with server-side Node.js runtimes — entirely through natural language prompting. Key capabilities include secure secrets management, npm package support, multiplayer app construction, and integrations with modern UI libraries like Framer Motion. The experience is power

items.title: VaultGemma: The World's Most Capable Differentially Private LLM — Google Research
items.author: research.google
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been highly active in research publications in early 2026. A standout development is the introduction of Aletheia, an autonomous AI math research agent that moves beyond competition-level problem solving to generate, verify, and revise mathematical proofs without human intervention — producing a fully AI-authored research paper (Feng26) on eigenweights in arithmetic geometry. Alongside this, DeepMind released 'Gemini Deep Think,' a model capability focused on accelerating mat

items.title: gemini-cli releases.md · google-gemini/gemini-cli
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization has seen significant activity in early 2026, with the most notable development being the release of Gemini 3.1 Pro and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview models in February 2026. These models support a context window of up to 1,048,576 tokens and accept text, image, PDF, and video inputs. The gemini-cli project received release note updates in January 2026 and has been actively developed with features including VSCode workspace integration, subagent local execution, san

items.title: Antigravity Is Google's New Agentic Development Platform - The New Stack
items.author: thenewstack.io
items.snippet: Google Antigravity, launched in November 2025 alongside Gemini 3, is Google's new agent-first development platform. It combines a traditional AI-powered IDE (Editor View) with a Manager Surface for orchestrating multiple autonomous agents. Agents can autonomously plan and execute tasks across the editor, terminal, and browser — launching local servers, running commands, and testing UIs. They produce Artifacts (task lists, plans, screenshots) for verification. Antigravity integrates Firebase for

items.title: Genkit Overview | Firebase
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework by Google/Firebase for building full-stack AI-powered applications using familiar code-centric patterns in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified interface for integrating AI models from providers like Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama, with built-in observability, type safety, and developer tooling. Key abstractions include 'flows' (special functions with observability and type safety), 'tools' (functions agents can call), a

items.title: Get Started with Firebase AI Logic SDKs
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Studio is Google's agentic, cloud-based development environment that enables developers to build and ship full-stack AI-powered applications. While not exclusively a game platform, it supports game creation through AI-assisted prototyping — developers can 'vibe-code' games using natural language prompts powered by Gemini, then deploy directly to Firebase Hosting. A dedicated codelab walks through building a kids' game on p5.js from scratch using Gemini prompts and publishing it live, ma
